description: Deep sky target taxonomy — traits drive processing decisions

galaxy_spiral.label: Spiral Galaxy
galaxy_spiral.processingNotes: Core protection critical (HDRMT ringing). IFN is a primary goal. Dust lanes need contrast. 3-zone masking: core (protect), arms (enhance), background+IFN (shadow lift). Headroom in stretch essential.

galaxy_edge_on.label: Edge-on Galaxy
galaxy_edge_on.processingNotes: Dust lane is THE feature — maximize contrast across the lane. No spiral arm color variation. Core vs halo masking. Brighten galaxy body with spatial mask + gamma. Focus on dust lane detail and galaxy halo extent, NOT IFN.

galaxy_elliptical.label: Elliptical Galaxy
galaxy_elliptical.processingNotes: Smooth profile — subtle detail enhancement only. Jets or shells in deep data. Uniform golden color.

galaxy_cluster.label: Galaxy Cluster
galaxy_cluster.processingNotes: Many tiny diverse galaxies. Background target DARKER than single galaxies (0.08-0.10 RGB). 3-scale LHE through soft masks (r=16-24 fine, r=35-64 medium, r=94-150 large) — medium scale is most useful. HDRMT with medianTransform=ON to prevent dark ringing in star-rich fields. NXT post-stretch 0.25-0.35 (NOT aggressive in linear — kills fine detail). Consider range mask selective stretch to brighten faint members without blowing cores. SXT may remove small galaxies — use conservative overlap. Color diversity across galaxies is a feature. LRGB lightness 0.45-0.55.

emission_nebula.label: Emission Nebula
emission_nebula.processingNotes: Ha signal dominates. Bright cores can clip — headroom needed. Faint outer regions need shadow lifting. Star control critical in rich fields. Color depends on workflow (HaRGB, SHO, HOO).

planetary_nebula.label: Planetary Nebula
planetary_nebula.processingNotes: Small subject — sharpness paramount. Internal structure (shells, knots) needs fine LHE. CORE BURNS EASILY — L stretch 0.25 max with headroom 0.10 (NOT 0.30). Outer halo extremely faint. Often OIII (blue/teal) + Ha (red) zoned color — process zones separately via masks. Ha injection MUST use luminance mask (clipLow=0.04-0.08) — without mask, background turns red. The faint outer veil needs Ha through SOFT mask.

reflection_nebula.label: Reflection Nebula
reflection_nebula.processingNotes: Blue scattered light IS the subject. Very faint — aggressive stretch. Star halos around illuminating star must be controlled. Natural blue, not cyan.

dark_nebula.label: Dark Nebula
dark_nebula.processingNotes: Subject IS the dark region — silhouette against star field. Over-stretching destroys it. Subtle dust texture via gentle LHE. Star colors in background are a feature.

supernova_remnant.label: Supernova Remnant
supernova_remnant.processingNotes: Filamentary structure is THE feature — fine LHE essential, avoid smoothing threads. Ha and OIII show different structures. Very faint outer filaments need aggressive shadow lifting.

star_cluster_globular.label: Globular Cluster
star_cluster_globular.processingNotes: Star resolution in core is the challenge. BXT critical. Core clips easily — headroom essential. Outer halo stars must be resolved. Star colors (red giants, blue stragglers) should be visible. SXT NOT recommended.

star_cluster_open.label: Open Cluster
star_cluster_open.processingNotes: Stars ARE the subject. SXT absolutely NOT recommended. Star color diversity is the primary aesthetic. Minimal processing — stretch, calibrate, gentle curves.

mixed_field.label: Mixed / Wide Field
mixed_field.processingNotes: Multiple object types in one field. Balance competing priorities. Regional processing attention. Color accuracy across nebula types.
